input_text: Spent the morning looking at the auth middleware. The class-based wrapper we inherited is breaking on async handlers because the constructor runs synchronously and the hooks can't await cleanly. Options: A) keep the class shape and add a separate async init step. B) migrate to function-based middleware with the composition pattern. B is more work upfront but the function style composes cleanly with existing async handlers. Decided: going with B. Will leave the class shim in place for one release so downstream callers can migrate incrementally. Also realised the JWT validator in auth/tokens.js wasn't checking the signing secret at module load — added a startup assertion. Don't think that counts as a decision, more a bug fix.

expected.text: Migrate auth middleware from class-based to function-based composition because the class constructor runs synchronously and hooks can't await cleanly
Leave the class shim in place for one release so downstream callers can migrate incrementally

reviewer_notes: Excluded the JWT startup assertion — it's a bug fix, not a decision. The author explicitly said so.
